nodes.typed_narration: The child notices the class clock ticking backward before anyone else reacts.
nodes.next_page_question: Why does the clock stop only when the child looks away?
nodes.final_hook_sentence: Then the clock writes the child's name in dust.
nodes.reader_retention_goal: Hold curiosity around the backward clock and the named warning.
nodes.visual_memory_point: a dusty classroom clock with reversed hands
technique_notes: page_turn_hook leaves a concrete unanswered question

nodes.typed_hook: The wrong detail becomes a rule.
nodes.typed_narration: Every time the child blinks, one more classmate forgets the child's name.
nodes.final_hook_sentence: The teacher still calls attendance, but the child's line is now blank.
nodes.reader_retention_goal: Keep the reader tracking how the rule is spreading.
nodes.visual_memory_point: an attendance sheet with one erased line

nodes.typed_hook: The ending reveals the rule was waiting from the start.
nodes.typed_narration: The child reaches home and sees the same attendance sheet on the kitchen table.
nodes.final_hook_sentence: The last blank line begins filling itself in.
nodes.reader_retention_goal: Leave the reader with a closed loop and one final unease.
nodes.visual_memory_point: the erased attendance line returning at home
technique_notes: fate_loop confirms recurrence without explaining everything
